About Manulife Global Fund - Asia Pacific REIT Fund
Manulife Global Fund - Asia Pacific REIT Fund seeks long-term capital appreciation and income generation by investing primarily through real estate investment trusts in the Asia Pacific ex-Japan region. The Fund will invest at least 70% of its net assets in REITs constituted in and/or traded in and/or primarily invested in underlying assets in the Asia Pacific ex-Japan region.

results.title: 2026 AP REITs Outlook: From Rate Relief to Growth Revival
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > **1. Organic Growth – Interest Cost Savings**
Content:
**Chart 3: MGF AP REIT Fund’s REIT holdings – Debt by fixed / floating-rate <sup>4</sup>**
**- In terms of the debt’s maturity:**
Around 43% of the Fund’s underlying debt is due for refinancing within the next two years, offering near-term interest savings **(Chart 4)** .
These rate tailwinds have started to materialise, as evidenced by the most recent quarterly earnings.
SREITs have seen an average decline of 20-30 bps in the cost of debt, year on year, as of the third quarter (Q3) of 2025, while most SREITs guided for further interest savings for the full year 2026 <sup>5</sup> .
As REITs continue to roll off their legacy, higher-cost debt, this provides room for DPU growth to resume over the next two years.
**Chart 4: MGF AP REIT Fund’s REIT holdings – Debt by maturity <sup>6</sup>**
 ... 
Section Title: 2026 Asia Pacific ex-Japan REITs outlook: From rate relief to growth revival > Summary: > Conclusion
Content:
AP REITs are entering a pivotal phase as interest rates decline, unlocking both organic and inorganic growth opportunities. Coupled with compelling yield spreads and supportive policy measures, AP REITs can offer an attractive risk/reward profile for investors seeking resilience and potential upside in a lower-rate environment. The investment team remains focused on active portfolio management to navigate uncertainty and capture growth in 2026 and beyond.
Download full PDF
**1** Source: Bloomberg, as of 30 November 2025. Note: Reference rates are: SORA (Singapore), EURIBOR (Eurozone), HIBOR (Hong Kong), BBSW (Australia), MIBOR (India), SONIA (UK), SOFR (USA), LPR (China).
**2** Source: Manulife Investment Management, company data, as of 30 November 2025. Note: Data covers the portfolio’s REITs holdings only and excludes fund managers/non-REIT holdings.

Section Title: Manulife Global Fund - Asia-Pacific REIT Fund P (USD) MDIST (G) > Objective
Content:
The Sub-Fund aims to provide long-term capital appreciation and income generation primarily through investment in real estate investment trusts (“REITs”) in the Asia Pacific ex-Japan region. Asia Pacific REIT Fund will invest at least 70% of its net assets in REITs constituted in and/or traded in and/or primarily invested in underlying assets in the Asia Pacific ex-Japan region, each of which is closed-ended and listed on any Regulated Market. The remaining assets of the Sub-Fund may be invested in real estate-related securities listed on any Regulated Market in the Asia Pacific ex-Japan region, closed-ended non-Asia Pacific ex-Japan REITs listed on any Regulated Market, and cash and cash equivalents.
More ▼
